documents.name: README.md
documents.excerpt:
**Project introduction:** This project aims to elucidate how
cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) in high-grade serous ovarian cancer
(HGSC) influence responses to emerging anti-cancer therapies. To achieve
this, we will develop a novel 3D ex vivo model integrating
patient-derived HGSC tumor cells, tumor-resident immune cells, and CAFs
to more accurately recapitulate the complexity of the tumor
mi

identity.project_summary: The aim is to create a novel 3D HGSC model through high-throughput drug testing.

**Project introduction:** This project aims to elucidate how
cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) in high-grade serous ovarian cancer
(HGSC) influence responses to emerging anti-cancer therapies. To achieve
this, we will develop a novel 3D ex vivo model integrating
patient-derived HGSC tumor cells, tumor-resident immune cells, and CAFs
to more accurately recapitulate the complexity of the tumor

Cancer -associated fibroblasts condition the ovarian cancer microenvironment and 
therapeutic responses to antibody -drug conjugates and immunotherapy 
 
María Hincapié -Otero1*, Nika Mikhailava¹, Kürşat Birgin¹, Aino Elomaa1,2, Sarah Wolf¹, Sara Palomino1,3, Iga 
Niemiec¹, Zhihan Liang¹, Aleksandra Shabanova¹, Joonas Jukonen1, Bassel Alsaed4, Tuula Salo5, Anni Virtanen1,6, 
Ulla-Maija Haltia1,7, Heidi Haikala4,8, Anniina Färkkilä1,3,7 
 
1 Research Program in Systems Oncology, University of Helsinki, Finland 
2 Institute of Biomedicine, Faculty of Medicine, University of Turku, Finland 
3 Institute for Molecular Medicine Finland, Helsinki Institute of Life Sciences, University of Helsinki, Finland 
4 Research Program in Translational Immunology, University of Helsinki, Finland 
5 Department of Oral and Maxillofacial Diseases, University of Helsinki, Helsinki, Finland 
6 Department of Pathology, University of Helsinki and HUS Diagnostic Center, Helsinki University Hospital, Helsinki, Finland 
7 Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology, Helsinki University Hospital, Finland 
8 Faculty of Medicine and Health Technology, Tampere University, Finland 
 
 
Correspondence should be addressed to A.F. ( anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi ) 
Introduction 
Cancer -associated fibroblasts (CAFs) are key regulators of the tumor microenvironment (TME), but how

should be addressed to A.F. ( anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi ) 
Introduction 
Cancer -associated fibroblasts (CAFs) are key regulators of the tumor microenvironment (TME), but how 
they drive immune evasion and shape responses to emerging therapies remains unclear. We combined 
spatial and single -cell transcriptomics with functional ex -vivo assays to define therapeutically relevant CAF 
phenotypes and assess their impact on anti -TROP2 antibody -drug conjugates (ADCs) and immunotherapy 
responses. 
Methodology 
We employed GeoMX spatial transcriptomics ( N = 40, AOIs = 705 ) of FFPE HGSC tumors to define spatially 
distinct fibroblast programs. We generated a tumor –stroma interface (TSI) –associated signature and 
projected it onto scRNA -seq data from HGSC tumors ( N = 29 ) to refine CAF states. We then scored each 
fibroblast based on signature expression and annotated them as TSI -like or stroma -like and performed 
pathway analyses in both datasets. We tested ADCs and immunotherapies in patient -derived 3D HGSC 
TME models +/ - CAFs to evaluate how CAF states modulate responses using scRNA -seq and flow 
cytometry. 
Results 
Spatial transcriptomics revealed a distinct TGFβ -driven CAF program enriched for extracellular matrix 
organization, cell adhesion, and tissue remodeling pathways at the TSI. Projection of the spatial gene

aled a distinct TGFβ -driven CAF program enriched for extracellular matrix 
organization, cell adhesion, and tissue remodeling pathways at the TSI. Projection of the spatial gene 
signature onto scRNA -seq data revealed that the TSI -CAFs expressed predominantly myCAF phenotypes. 
Spatial immune deconvolution showed that TSI -CAFs associated with significantly decreased infiltration of 
NK cells, B cells, CD8 ⁺ and CD4 ⁺ T cells suggesting an immune -excluded interface driven by CAFs. This 
spatial immune -exclusion was also associated with higher expression of TROP2 in cancer cells, providing 
a rationale for TROP2 -targeted ADC -immunotherapy combination strategies. To functionally assess how 
TSI-CAFs modulate therapeutic responses to these age nts ex -vivo, we established and validated a modular

3D HGSC TME model enabling controlled inclusion or exclusion of CAFs, allowing evaluation of responses 
to anti -TROP2 ADCs and immunotherapies. 
Conclusion 
myCAFs create an immune -suppressed TSI associated with higher ADC target expression. This highlights 
the importance of combining ADCs with immunotherapies to re -activate HGSC anti -tumor immunity.

**Spatial Transcriptomics Reveals Spatially Distinct Fibroblast Gene
Signatures in High-Grade Serous Carcinoma**

Nika Mikhailava¹, María Hincapié-Otero¹, Joonas Jukonen¹, Iga Niemiec¹,
Zhihan Liang¹, Aleksandra Shabanova¹, Matilda Salko¹, Ulla-Maija
Haltia¹², Anni Virtanen¹³, Anniina Färkkilä¹²³⁴

¹ Research Program in Systems Oncology, University of Helsinki,
Helsinki, Finland\
² Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology, Department of Oncology,
Clinical Trials Unit, Comprehensive Cancer Center, Helsinki University
Hospital, Helsinki, Finland\
³ Department of Pathology, University of Helsinki and HUS Diagnostic
Center, Helsinki University Hospital, Helsinki, Finland\
⁴ Institute for Molecular Medicine Finland, Helsinki Institute for Life
Sciences, University of Helsinki, Helsinki, Finland

**Introduction**

High-grade serous carcinoma (HGSC) is the most common subtype of ovarian
cancer and the deadliest gynecological malignancy. Although most
patients initially respond to platinum-based neoadjuvant chemotherapy
(NACT), resistance typically develops, with relapse rates of 70--80%.
The tumor microenvironment (TME), particularly cancer-associated
fibroblasts (CAFs), contributes to resistance. Targeting CAF-derived
pathways offers a novel potential strategy for platinum-resistant
patients.

**Methods**

Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) post-NACT tumor samples (n =

rgeting CAF-derived
pathways offers a novel potential strategy for platinum-resistant
patients.

**Methods**

Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) post-NACT tumor samples (n =
40) from HGSC patients enrolled in the Oncosys-OVA clinical trial
(NCTXXYY) were profiled using GeoMx spatial transcriptomics.
Fibroblast-specific gene expression was deconvoluted using the
BayesPrism algorithm.

**Results**

A total of 611 stroma-localized Areas of Illumination (AOIs) were
analyzed (tumor--stroma interface \[TSI\] = 309, distant stroma = 302).
Hierarchical clustering of the most variable genes revealed candidate
signatures distinguishing TSI from distant stroma. CAFs in the two
compartments displayed distinct transcriptional profiles, with the most
variable genes showing higher expression in distant stroma. These
distant-stroma-enriched genes were linked to extracellular matrix (ECM)
organization, cell adhesion, and tissue remodeling, suggesting a
matrix-constructive phenotype. Hierarchical clustering reinforced the
spatial segregation of CAF transcriptional programs, outlining potential
targets for overcoming drug resistance in HGSC.

**Conclusions and perspectives**

Our analysis identified potential fibroblast targets to address drug
resistance and underscored the value of spatial profiling in resolving
differences between distant and TSI fibroblasts. Future work will focus

l fibroblast targets to address drug
resistance and underscored the value of spatial profiling in resolving
differences between distant and TSI fibroblasts. Future work will focus
on functional validation of selected candidates in immune-competent
patient-derived cell (icPDC) co-culture models to determine their roles
in tumor progression and therapy response.

Introduction
 
Drug
 
resistance
 
in
 
high-grade
 
serous
 
ovarian
 
cancer
 
(HGSC)
 
is
 
strongly
 
influenced
 
by
 
the
 
tumor
 
microenvironment
 
(TME).
 
However,
 
conventional
 
2D
 
models
 
used
 
for
 
drug
 
testing
 
typically
 
include
 
only

C)
 
is
 
strongly
 
influenced
 
by
 
the
 
tumor
 
microenvironment
 
(TME).
 
However,
 
conventional
 
2D
 
models
 
used
 
for
 
drug
 
testing
 
typically
 
include
 
only
 
one
 
or
 
two
 
cell
 
types
 
and
 
lack
 
the
 
three-dimensional
 
architecture
 
needed
 
to
 
capture
 
essential
 
tumor-stroma
 
and
 
extracellular
 
matrix
 
(ECM)
 
interactions,
 
limiting
 
their
 
predictive
 
value.
 
Patient-derived
 
3D
 
cultures
 
provide
 
a
 
more
 
physiologically
 
relevant
 
platform
 
to
 
study
 
drug
 
responses
 
and
 
identify
 
predictive
 
biomarkers.
 
Here,
 
we
 
present
 
a
 
novel
 
3D
 
HGSC
 
model
 
incorporating
 
patient-derived
 
cancer
 
cells,
 
tumor-resident
 
immune
 
cells,
 
and
 
cancer-associated
 
fibroblasts
 
(CAFs),
 
embedded
 
in
 
a
 
human
 
omentum-derived
 
ECM,
 
to
 
better
 
recapitulate
 
tumor
 
complexity
 
and
 
improve
 
drug
 
testing
 
approaches.
 
Materials
 
and
 
Methods
 
Tissue
 
samples
 
were
 
obtained
 
from
 
patients
 
enrolled
 
in
 
the
 
ONCOSYS-OVA
 
clinical
 
trial
 
(NCT06117384).
 
To
 
optimize
 
the
 
isolation
 
of
 
cancer,
 
immune,
 
and
 
non-immune
 
stromal
 
cells,
 
we
 
tested
 
three
 
enzymatic
 
dissociation
 
methods:
 
(i)
 
dispase,
 
(ii)
 
a
 
collagenase-hyaluronidase
 
(CH)
 
cocktail,
 
and
 
(iii)
 
sequential
 
dispase
 
treatment
 
followed
 
by
 
the
 
cocktail
 
on

tion
 
methods:
 
(i)
 
dispase,
 
(ii)
 
a
 
collagenase-hyaluronidase
 
(CH)
 
cocktail,
 
and
 
(iii)
 
sequential
 
dispase
 
treatment
 
followed
 
by
 
the
 
cocktail
 
on
 
the
 
remaining
 
tissue.
 
The
 
optimal
 
method,
 
selected
 
based
 
on
 
cell
 
recovery
 
and
 
viability,
 
yielded
 
two
 
fractions:
 
one
 
enriched
 
in
 
cancer
 
and
 
immune
 
cells,
 
and
 
another
 
containing
 
also
 
non-immune
 
stromal
 
cells.
 
The
 
latter
 
was
 
further
 
purified
 
using
 
magnetic-activated
 
cell
 
sorting
 
(MACS).
 
The
 
purified
 
stromal
 
cells
 
were
 
recombined
 
with
 
the
 
cancer
 
and
 
immune
 
cell
 
fraction
 
in
 
a
 
1:1
 
ratio,
 
embedded
 
in
 
an
 
omentum-derived
 
ECM,
 
and
 
cultured
 
under
 
serum-free
 
conditions
 
for
 
up
 
to
 
nine
 
days.
 
Model
 
characterization
 
included
 
flow
 
cytometry
 
for
 
phenotyping
 
and
 
functional
 
assessment,
 
and
 
immunofluorescence
 
to
 
evaluate
 
cellular
 
composition
 
and
 
viability.
 
Further
 
cell-type
 
identification
 
and
 
transcriptional
 
profiles
 
will
 
be
 
assessed
 
by
 
scRNA-seq.
 
 
Results
 
and
 
Discussion
 
Optimization
 
of
 
the
 
tissue
 
dissociation
 
allowed
 
efficient
 
recovery
 
of
 
key
 
cellular
 
components.
 
The
 
sequential
 
method
 
effectively
 
separated
 
distinct
 
cell
 
populations
 
into
 
two
 
fractions.
 
Dispase

fficient
 
recovery
 
of
 
key
 
cellular
 
components.
 
The
 
sequential
 
method
 
effectively
 
separated
 
distinct
 
cell
 
populations
 
into
 
two
 
fractions.
 
Dispase
 
treatment
 
improved
 
the
 
recovery
 
of
 
cancer
 
cells
 
while
 
also
 
yielding
 
immune
 
cells
 
with
 
lower
 
stress-response
 
gene
 
expression
 
compared
 
to
 
those
 
isolated
 
with
 
CH
 
alone.
 
Subsequent
 
CH
 
treatment
 
significantly
 
enhanced

the
 
recovery
 
of
 
CAFs.
 
After
 
culturing
 
the
 
cells
 
in
 
the
 
3D
 
model,
 
flow
 
cytometry
 
confirmed
 
that
 
the
 
cellular
 
composition
 
remained
 
stable
 
over
 
time
 
and
 
retained
 
functional
 
characteristics.
 
Immunofluorescence
 
stainings
 
also
 
demonstrated
 
the
 
persistence
 
of
 
key
 
cell
 
populations
 
within
 
the
 
cultures,
 
supporting
 
the
 
model’s
 
structural
 
and
 
phenotypic
 
integrity.
 
Conclusion
 
We
 
developed
 
a
 
novel
 
3D
 
model
 
of
 
HGSC
 
that
 
successfully
 
incorporates
 
key
 
components
 
of
 
the
 
HGSC
 
TME,
 
providing
 
a
 
promising
 
platform
 
to
 
study
 
cell-cell
 
interactions
 
and
 
patient-specific
 
drug
 
responses.

Introduction
 
Chemotherapy
 
resistance
 
in
 
high-grade
 
serous
 
ovarian
 
cancer
 
(HGSC)
 
is
 
strongly
 
influenced
 
by
 
the
 
tumor
 
microenvironment
 
(TME)
 
where
 
tumor-host
 
interactions
 
are
 
critical.
 
Conventional
 
2D
 
models
 
lack
 
this
 
complexity,
 
limiting
 
their
 
predictive
 
value.
 
To
 
address
 
this,
 
we
 
developed
 
a
 
patient-derived
 
3D
 
culture
 
model
 
incorporating
 
cancer,
 
immune
 
cells,
 
and
 
cancer-associated
 
fibroblasts
 
(CAFs)
 
embedded
 
in
 
human
 
omentum-derived
 
extracellular
 
matrix
 
(ECM),
 
capturing
 
the
 
TME
 
architecture
 
and
 
diversity.
 
In
 
this
 
proof-of-concept
 
study
 
we
 
applied
 
multiplexed
 
single-cell
 
RNA
 
sequencing
 
(scRNA-seq)
 
to
 
compare
 
drug-treated
 
cultures
 
with
 
and
 
without
 
CAFs
 
to
 
uncover
 
how
 
CAFs
 
shape
 
treatment
 
responses.

Samples
 
were
 
collected
 
within
 
the
 
Oncosys-OVA
 
study
 
(NCT06117384),
 
and
 
processed
 
using
 
an
 
optimized
 
enzymatic
 
dissociation
 
protocol,
 
isolating
 
cancer,
 
immune
 
cells,
 
and
 
CAFs.
 
We
 
generated
 
two
 
patient-matched
 
cultures:

The

Results
 
and
 
Discussion
 
Using
 
flow
 
cytometry
 
and
 
immunofluorescence,
 
we
 
confirmed
 
that
 
both
 
CAF–
 
and
 
CAF+
 
3D
 
cultures
 
maintained
 
viability,

composition,
 
and

functional
 
characteristics
 
of
 
the
 
TME
 
during
 
culture,

controlled
 
comparisons
 
of
 
drug
 
responses
 
with
 
or
 
without
 
CAF
 
influence.
 
Multiplexed
 
scRNA-seq
 
yielded
 
high-quality
 
transcriptomes
 
across

and

analysis

the
 
feasibility
 
of
 
applying
 
scRNA-seq
 
to
 
complex,
 
fully
 
humanized

across

Conclusion
 
We
 
present
 
a
 
proof-of-concept
 
application
 
of

to

relevant

HGSC

**Contrast Narrow (0.4--0.6)**: Increases contrast in midrange but may
clip bright/dark regions.

**Contrast Wide (0.2--0.8)**: Captures more intensity range but appears
flatter.

**Gamma \< 1 (0.5)**: Brightens midtones --- highlights dim areas.

**Gamma \> 1 (1.5)**: Darkens midtones --- emphasizes bright regions.

Data analysis #5 (meeting 24.06.2025)

**PDGFRB and PDGFRA:**

Some studies report that **high stromal PDGFRB** correlates with **poor
prognosis**, but findings are **inconsistent**.

Based on IHC of PDGFRB and PDGFRA in stromal and tumor compartments in
HGSC, PDGFRA and PDGFRB **doesn't have** prognostic benefit in HGSC
\[1\].

**FAP:**

**High FAP⁺ fibroblast abundance** alone **was not** associated with
shorter survival **in the whole cohort** \[2\].

**However**, when **both CD8⁺ T-cell density and FAP⁺ fibroblast levels
were high**, outcomes were **significantly worse**:

- In the **discovery cohort**:

 - High FAP + high CD8 → **shorter PFS** (11.4 vs. 18.6 months, *p*
 > = 0.01)

- In the **validation cohort**:

 - High FAP + high CD8 → **shorter OS** (31.5 vs. 76.9 months, *p*
 > = 0.02)

### **Spatial Immune Exclusion in TSI and Distant Stroma**

Analysis of **cell abundances** in the TSI and distant stroma segments
reveals **spatial exclusion of immune cells**, likely mediated by

une Exclusion in TSI and Distant Stroma**

Analysis of **cell abundances** in the TSI and distant stroma segments
reveals **spatial exclusion of immune cells**, likely mediated by
**myCAFs** present in the TSI. Specifically, the proportions of **B
cells, NK cells, dendritic cells, CD8⁺ and CD4⁺ T cells, and endothelial
cells** are significantly reduced in the TSI.

This analysis and corresponding plot were performed **for IDS samples
only**.

![](media/image68.png){width="6.5in" height="4.333333333333333in"}

**TROP2 in TME**

The following analysis was performed on both PDS and IDS samples.\
First, we conducted a basic exploration to determine the cellular source
of **TROP2 expression**. Our results indicate that TROP2 expression is
**restricted to tumor cells**. Specifically:

- TROP2 expression is significantly **higher in tumor AOIs** compared
 > to non-tumor regions.

- There is a **positive correlation** between the proportion of tumor
 > cells within an AOI and TROP2 expression levels.

- Additionally, **deconvolution analysis** confirms that the signal
 > attributed to tumor cells is substantially higher than that from
 > other cell types.

These findings suggest that **TROP2 is predominantly expressed by tumor
cells** within the tumor microenvironment.

e findings suggest that **TROP2 is predominantly expressed by tumor
cells** within the tumor microenvironment.

![](media/image70.png){width="6.5in" height="4.333333333333333in"}

### **TROP2 and Immune Cell Proportions in Tumor AOIs**

TROP2 expression is associated with **lower proportions of immune
cells** within tumor AOIs. Overall, the proportions of various immune
cell types are **inversely correlated** with TROP2 expression,
suggesting that tumor cells may **spatially exclude immune cells** from
the tumor microenvironment.

An important avenue for further investigation is the potential
relationship between **TROP2 expression in tumor cells** and **immune
exhaustion**, which may provide additional insight into tumor-immune
interaction

**Experimental Plan for Multiplexed scRNA-seq (Main Discussion, \~40
min)**

**Technical Considerations & Requirements:**

- **There is a new scRNA-seq method** that allows loading **up to
 > 40,000 cells if needed**; standard is 10,000.

- Goal: load **30,000 cells**, to sequence **20,000**.

- Start with **50,000--100,000 cells/condition**.

- Ideal input for the droplet generation: **150,000 cells total**, to
 > account for possible losses and errors during the prep.

- **Cell concentration**: \~1700--2000 live cells/µL.

**Multiplexing Strategy**

neration: **150,000 cells total**, to
 > account for possible losses and errors during the prep.

- **Cell concentration**: \~1700--2000 live cells/µL.

**Multiplexing Strategy**

**Hashtag C TotalSeq antibodies** support 5\' end sequencing (TCR/BCR
compatible).

- TotalSeq A, B, and C available; C fits 5' end sequencing.

- Antibodies \~450 EUR/antibody; use **2 µL per 10⁶ cells/sample**.

- See: [[BioLegend
 > TotalSeq]{.underline}](https://www.biolegend.com/nl-nl/totalseq/single-cell-rna/multiplex-samples-and-hashtags)

- **Including TCR sequencing does not increase the costs dramatically,
 > might be a very nice option to consider for an extra layer of
 > information.**

- **Number of multiplexed conditions**: 3--5 per sample preferred
 > (Satu multiplexes up to 10 but with cell lines).

- **Avoid using UMI barcode #11** due to known issues.

- **Dead cell removal is recommended**, especially if viability is
 > \~40--50%.

- However, they\'ve successfully run 20--40% viability samples.

- **Fixation of hashed cells**: not tested/validated in their setup.

- It might be possible to pool patients when multiplexing different
 > treatment conditions using either 1) SNPs for patient
 > demultiplexing or 2) UMIs (since the number of treatments will not
 > exceed the number of available UMI-labelled antibodies combos).

nditions using either 1) SNPs for patient

 > best for us, especially if we can multiplex patients with the

Change the experimental design to include TWO ADCs, for that **we will
exclude chemo and talabostat from the original experimental design**

There are two options:

1\) include two anti-TROP2 antibodies with different payloads or

2\) include one anti-TROP2 and one anti-FRa

There are risks associated to both of these options:

1\) Maybe we don't see a significant difference between the two
conditions.

2\) FRa-targeted ADCs have only proven effective in selected patients
with very high FRa expression. Since our pilot is a cohort of only 4
patients, the risk that only one is FRa high is quite high, and
therefore we would be testing the therapy mostly in patients where it
wouldn't be effective

If we don't see a lot of differences between the two anti-TROP ADCs, the
results would still be publishable, as we can report that they still
work and that the effects on the TME cells are x and y. On the other
hand, we could also test anti-FRa and see if the combination with ICI is
synergistic, which is also interesting and publishable. However, this
seems more risky. Also, the clinical relevance would be higher if we
test anti-TROP2 because anyways the anti-FRa is only approved and most

eresting and publishable. However, this
seems more risky. Also, the clinical relevance would be higher if we
test anti-TROP2 because anyways the anti-FRa is only approved and most
likely reimbursed to patients that are selected through the FRa
biomarker (very high expression), so our results most likely wouldn't
change this. **We will then include both of the anti-TROP2 ADCs, and
keep the anti-FRa as backup**

We can still do flow cytometry paired with the scRNA-seq and look at the
immune cell activation upon treatment as we are also testing the
possible synergy of ADCs in combination with immunotherapy.

**We need to determine the concentrations of the ADCs we want to use. To
do this, we will perform titration experiments.** Our primary endpoint
is cell survival. Although the goal of ADCs in patients is to kill
cancer cells, in our experiments we need to identify the concentration
at which the drug begins to have an effect, but the cells still survive
until the readout.

#### Notes

**Experimental Plan for Multiplexed scRNA-seq (Main Discussion, \~40
min)**

**Technical Considerations & Requirements:**

- **There is a new scRNA-seq method** that allows loading **up to
 > 40,000 cells if needed**; standard is 10,000.

- Goal: load **30,000 cells**, to sequence **20,000**.

- Start with **50,000--100,000 cells/condition**.

- Ideal input for the droplet generation: **150,000 cells total**, to

.

- Goal: load **30,000 cells**, to sequence **20,000**.

- Start with **50,000--100,000 cells/condition**.

- Ideal input for the droplet generation: **150,000 cells total**, to
 > account for possible losses and errors during the prep.

- **Cell concentration**: \~1700--2000 live cells/µL.

**Multiplexing Strategy**

**Hashtag C TotalSeq antibodies** support 5\' end sequencing (TCR/BCR
compatible).

- TotalSeq A, B, and C available; C fits 5' end sequencing.

- Antibodies \~450 EUR/antibody; use **2 µL per 10⁶ cells/sample**.

- See: [[BioLegend
 > TotalSeq]{.underline}](https://www.biolegend.com/nl-nl/totalseq/single-cell-rna/multiplex-samples-and-hashtags)

- **Including TCR sequencing does not increase the costs dramatically,
 > might be a very nice option to consider for an extra layer of
 > information.**

- **Number of multiplexed conditions**: 3--5 per sample preferred
 > (Satu multiplexes up to 10 but with cell lines).

- **Avoid using UMI barcode #11** due to known issues.

- **Dead cell removal is recommended**, especially if viability is
 > \~40--50%.

- However, they\'ve successfully run 20--40% viability samples.

- **Fixation of hashed cells**: not tested/validated in their setup.

- It might be possible to pool patients when multiplexing different
 > treatment conditions using either 1) SNPs for patient

hashed cells**: not tested/validated in their setup.

- It might be possible to pool patients when multiplexing different
 > treatment conditions using either 1) SNPs for patient
 > demultiplexing or 2) UMIs (since the number of treatments will not
 > exceed the number of available UMI-labelled antibodies combos).

- **Alternative multiplexing** method available: **chip-/bead-based**,
 > less time-consuming than hashing. However, hashing might be the
 > best for us, especially if we can multiplex patients with the
 > UMI-labelled antibodies as well.

**Sample processing strategy**

- Cell counts should be done before and after hashing and preparation.

- Viability check is critical before proceeding.

**Cost Estimates & Logistics**

- Pilot study is highly encouraged (e.g. 3 patients ± fibroblasts).
 > Ideally, also include the drugs that will be tested to get a sense

well and with 800 ul media per well. No digestion was used.

isolated and attached to the plate. Wells were washed once with PDB and

Media was changed every Mon, Wed, and Fri. Before the media change,
wells were washed once with PBS.

**02.07.2024 - Day 15**

**Coverslip preparation**

**A**utoclaved coverslips were placed in the wells of a 24-well plate. 6
wells were coated with Cultrex gel solution, 4 wells were coated with
Omentum gel solution, and 6 wells were left uncoated. All reagents were
kept on ice during preparation.

The final concentration of both gels was determined to be 0.5 mg/ml. See
the calculation for gel solutions below.

Cultrex gel solution was prepared by mixing media and Cultrex gel stock
and left on ice for a few minutes before adding it to the wells. 400 µl
of Cultrex gel solution was added per well. Cultrex gel solution needs
to incubate overnight at +37°C, and the coating solution can be removed
just before cell seeding.

Omentum gel solution was prepared by first adding omentum stock,
aprotinin, and thrombin to the media. After fibrinogen was thawed and
warmed in a +37°C water bath and everything was ready for plating the
omentum gel solution, fibrinogen was added to the solution.

We tried adding 400 µl of Omentum gel solution per well, but it was too
much as it covered the whole well, not just the coverslip. We did new
calculations (seen below) and added 100 µl of Omentum gel solution per
well. That was enough to cover the coverslip with a thin layer of the
gel. However, even less could be enough.

alculations (seen below) and added 100 µl of Omentum gel solution per
well. That was enough to cover the coverslip with a thin layer of the
gel. However, even less could be enough. After 1 hour of incubation at
+37°C, medium was added to the Omentum gel-coated wells.

400 µl PBS was added to the uncoated coverslips/wells.

Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads protocol by Miltenyi Biotec was used as
guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared in advance: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA) to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and everything else was sterile
except for EDTA.

ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice when not needed inside
the hood.

After calculations cells were centrifuged at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.
The supernatant was aspirated and the cell pellet was resuspended in 80
µl of cold MACS buffer (commercial protocol suggests 80 µl per 10 E7
cells). To the suspension, 20 µl of Anti-Fibroblast MicroBeads was added
(protocol: 20 µl per 10 E7 cells) Cell suspension was mixed well and
incubated 15 min at +4C (protocol: 30 min at RT). Cells were washed by
adding 1 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300 g for 10 min at +4C. The
supernatant was aspirated and cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS
buffer.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.
Column and filter were prepared by washing it with 3 ml MACS buffer. The
cell suspension was added to the column and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS
buffer. Collect flow-through to a separate 15 ml falcon tube, this time
cell suspension and washing were collected into the same falcon tube.
Now this suspension should include all cells except fibroblasts i.e.
epithelial cells.

The column was removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml
falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly
pushing the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells flushed
out.

Both cell suspensions were centrifuged 300 x for 10 min and then
resuspended in 1 ml media. Cell counts were calculated:

Fibroblasts: 0.7 E7 cells/ml

Epithelial cells 0.407 E7 cells/ml

**26.07.2024 - day 39**

**IF staining of PFA fixed cells in coverslips**

Firstly, prepared Blocking buffer (3 % BSA and 2 % TritonX-100 in 1xPBS)
by adding 0.3 g of BSA and 0.2 ml TritonX-100 into 10 ml 1xPBS. To
enable the pipeting of triton, cutted the smalles part of a pipet tip
off. Vortex the solution thoroughly until BSA and triton was dissolved.

Prepared a IF chamber with wet tissue papers and parafilm, and added
coverslips on top to the parafilm. Added 50 µl of the blocking buffer
per coverslip and incubated 30 min RT shielded from light. Removed
blocking buffer by converting the coverslip and tipeting the buffer from
the edge of the coverslip and from the parafilm.

Prepared antibodymix with 1.75 µl PAX8, 1.167 µl FAP and 0.12 µl aSMA
antibodies in 350 µl blocking buffer. Added 50 µl of the antibodymix to
the coverslip and incubated 45 min RT shielded from light. Removed the
antibody by converting the coverslip and washed them by pipetting 100 µl

. Added 50 µl of the antibodymix to
the coverslip and incubated 45 min RT shielded from light. Removed the
antibody by converting the coverslip and washed them by pipetting 100 µl
PBS onto the coverslips while folding them converted. Prepared HOECHST
antibodymix with 35 µl of 1:100 diluted HOECHST mix into 350 µl blocking
buffer. Added 50 µl of HOECHST mix per coverslip and incubated 5 min.

After incubation, washed the coverslips by pipetting 100 µl PBS onto the
coverslips while folding them converted. Removed excess PBS by turning
the coverslip sideways on a paper tissue. Mounted the coverslips by
adding 20 µl glycerol onto the coverslip and placing the coverslips on
to microscopy slides.

Surface staining:

Added FACS buffer and centrifuged 400 g +4 C 6 min.

Made an antibody mix for the multicolor samples; 50µl per tube, diluted
in Brilliant Stain buffer. For FMO samples, added the needed amount
Brilliant Stain buffer (see table below) to the cells and then added
antibodies one by one to the tubes (containing the cells). For FMO
samples, every other antibody was added except the corresponding one.

Incubated all samples 30 min at +4C and an additional 10 min RT. Washed
and incubated for 40 min with Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) and

dded except the corresponding one.

Incubated all samples 30 min at +4C and an additional 10 min RT. Washed
and incubated for 40 min with Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) and
added 1x permeabilization (10x perm (Invitrogen) buffer diluted in
mQH2O) for 5 min. Centrifuged and resuspended the pellets in FACS buffer
for overnight storage at +4C.

**In addition to all described above, Panos established long-term
organoid cultures of the cells.**

**16.7.24 - day 1**

**FACS staining with fibroblast flow cytometry panel: intracellular
staining and compensations**

Intracellular staining:

Centrifuged the cells 400 g 6min +4C and prepared the intracellular
antibody mix:

15 µl APC a-SMA in 585 µl 1 x Permeabilization buffer.

Resuspended the pellet in 50 µl of intracellular antibody mix or only
permeabilization buffer and incubated 30 min +4C and an additional 10
min RT shielded from light.

After incubation, washed the cells with 1 x Permeabilization buffer,
centrifuged 400 g 6 min +4C, and resuspended the pellet in 400 µl FACS
buffer. Pipeted the cell suspension through Falcon 5 ml polystyrene tube
with a cell-strainer cap (blue).

Samples were kept on ice and protected from light until running at
Fortessa.

Compensation beads:

Added 1 drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG,
#51-90-9001229) and negative (CompBeads Negative Control #51-90-9001291)

ntil running at
Fortessa.

Compensation beads:

Added 1 drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG,

compensation beads to Falcon 5ml polystyrene round bottom tubes (white
cap). To each compensation tube labeled for a single

## Flow cytometry results

Some cells are outside of the scale but the dissociation worked better
(compared to S464_pOvaR) and we can clearly see more stromal cells in
the scatter plot. From EpCAM-, CD45- and CD31- cells, plots with CD29 to
FAP and a-SMA to CD29 show similar patter as seen in Givel et al paper

n clearly see more stromal cells in
the scatter plot. From EpCAM-, CD45- and CD31- cells, plots with CD29 to
FAP and a-SMA to CD29 show similar patter as seen in Givel et al paper
[[https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-018-03348-z]{.underline}](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-018-03348-z).

Conclusion is that FSC and SSC voltages need to be smaller. Maybe FSC
150 to 100 and SSC 230 to 210.

HGSOC by FACS. Results from a representative HGSOC patient are shown.
Cells isolated from freshly dissociated human HGSOC were first gated on
DAPI−, EPCAM−, CD45−, CD31− cells, for excluding dead cells (DAPI+),
epithelial cells (EPCAM+), hematopoietic cells (CD45+), and endothelial
cells (CD31+). Selected cells were next examined using six fibroblast
markers. Representative flow cytometry plots show gating strategies
based on FAP, CD29, SMA, and FSP1 that allow the identification of four
sub-populations of fibroblasts in HGSOC: CAF-S1 are CD29Med FAPHigh

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples and washed the larger tumor pieces
 > by placing them into a 50 ml Falcon tube, pouring sterile HBSS on
 > top, and gently inverting the tube several times.

- Approx. ⅔ of the tumor pieces were sliced and divided into two 50 ml
 > Falcon tubes for conditions 1&2 and 3&4.

- The remaining ⅓ of the tumor pieces were minced into smaller pieces
 > using a sterile scalpel and scissors, and then transferred into a
 > 50 ml Falcon tube for conditions 5 and 6.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated 1&2 and 3&4 with 0.125 % trypsin for 20 min in +37°C water
 > bath.

 > 10 ml 0.25% trypsin EDTA

 - Gently inverted the tubes a few times after 10 min and at the
 > end of the incubation period.

- After incubation, took the tissue pieces out and placed them into

rypsin EDTA

 > together and marked this cell suspension as sample 1-4.

**Fibroblast purification**

Tried again fibroblast purification for cells fron conditions 1-5 pooled
together. Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads protocol by Miltenyi Biotec was
used as guidance.

Used the previously prepared MACS buffer, but re- sterile-filtered
before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice when not needed inside
the hood.

After calculations cells were centrifuged at 300 g for 5 min RT. The
supernatant was aspirated and the cell pellet was resuspended in 80 µl
of cold MACS buffer (Commercial protocol suggests 80 µl per 10 E7 cells.
Our cell count was much less than E7, but decided to try this for now
and next time could decrease the volume of the buffer and beads.) To the
suspension, 20 µl of Anti-Fibroblast MicroBeads was added (protocol: 20
µl per 10 E7 cells) Cell suspension was mixed well and incubated 30 min
at RT. Cells were washed by adding 1 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300
g for 10 min. The supernatant was aspirated and cells were resuspended
in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.

in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

cell suspension was added to the column and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS
buffer. Collected the flow-through to a 15 ml falcon tube which should
include all cells except fibroblasts. The column was removed from the
separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was
added to the column and with firmly pushing the plunger into the column,
magnetically labeled cells flushed out. Both cell suspensions were
centrifuged 300 g for 10 min, and resuspended in 500 µl of DMEM/F12+++
media.

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

- Condition 1: 30 min 0.125 % trypsin and 1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

- Condition 6: Dispase for 30 min, followed by C&H for 1 hour

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples and washed the larger tumor pieces
 > by placing them into a 50 ml Falcon tube, pouring sterile HBSS on
 > top, and gently inverting the tube several times.

- The tumor piece was cut in three adjacent parts

- Two pieces were added into a 50 ml Falcon tube containing 20 mL of
 > 0.125 % trypsin in PBS for conditions 1 and 3.

- The remaining piece was minced into small pieces using a sterile
 > scalpel and scissors, and then transferred into a 50 mL Falcon
 > tube for condition 6.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- For condition 1&3, treated with 0.125 % trypsin for 30 min in +37°C
 > water bath.

dition 6.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- For condition 1&3, treated with 0.125 % trypsin for 30 min in +37°C
 > water bath.

 > 10 ml 0.25% trypsin EDTA

 - Gently inverted the tubes a few times every 10 min and at the
 > end of the incubation period.

- After incubation, took the tissue pieces out and placed them into a
 > cell culture dishes for mincing.

 > 1000 rpm 10 min +4C, did RBC lysis (2 ml of lysis buffer, 5 min
 > incuction and diluted with 8 ml of media) and resuspended in 1 ml

- Minced the sliced tissue pieces into tiny pieces, and divided the

 > 3).

- Treated samples with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media per condition: 4.45 ml DMEM/F12+++
 > media + 500 µl C&H (10x stock) + 50 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples with a 70 µm cell strainer to obtain
 > single-cell suspensions

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transferred the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, washed the stainer with 5 ml media, and discarded the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

ion\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

 > strainer

 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon

 > leftover tissue pieces.

- RBC lysis: 4 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 20 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media and made a 1:10 dilution for
 > counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry, and freezing.

3\. \*Fibroblast enrichment\*:

Rest of dispase + C&H dissociated cells was used for fibroblast
enrichment. The total cell count was 42.2 E6 cells before starting the
protocol.

Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads protocol by Miltenyi Biotec was used as
guidance.

Used the previously prepared MACS buffer, but re- sterile-filtered
before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice when not needed inside
the hood.

Cells were centrifuged at 300 g for 5 min RT and the cell pellet was
resuspended in 80 µl of cold MACS buffer (Commercial protocol suggests
80 µl per 10 E7 cells. To the suspension, 20 µl of Anti-Fibroblast
MicroBeads was added (protocol: 20 µl per 10 E7 cells) Cell suspension
was mixed well and incubated 30 min at RT. Cells were washed by adding 1
ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300 g for 10 min. The supernatant was
aspirated and cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

ed 30 min at RT. Cells were washed by adding 1
ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300 g for 10 min. The supernatant was
aspirated and cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.
Column and filter were prepared by washing it with 3 ml MACS buffer. The
cell suspension was added to the column and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS
buffer. Collected the flow-through to a 15 ml falcon tube which should
include all cells except fibroblasts. The column was removed from the
separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was
added to the column and with firmly pushing the plunger into the column,
magnetically labeled cells flushed out. Both cell suspensions were
centrifuged 300 g for 10 min, and resuspended in 500 µl of DMEM/F12+++
media.

# 4.10.2024

**S051_pOme P16 (⅓ P15, 23.9.2024 JJ) → S051_pOme P17 (⅓ P16, 4.10.2024
AE)**

Splitted the organoids according to the protocol "Short format organoid
passaging protocol".

Splitted 2 wells into 3 wells each, and discarded the 3rd well. In total
6 wells.

While splitting, kept the organoids from 2 different wells separately.
Prepared also 2 vials with diluted Cultrex: to achieve 7.5 mg/ml
concentration of Cultrex, diluted 481 µl of stock (11.7 mg/ml) into 269
µl cold DPBS. Both diluted Cultrex solutions should have contained over
750 µl but when pipetting took everything (which was under 750 µl) and
added that into the cell pellet. Made 3 x 10 droplets of the first
detached well and 3 x 11 droplets of the second well. The pellet of
second well was big compared to the one from first.

Incubated the droplets for 45 min and in meantime prepared 19 ml of M1
media with 9.5 µl ROCKinhibitor and warmed that. Lastly, added 3 ml of
media to each well.

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

- Washed big tissue pieces twice with HBSS.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).

ased on cell count, decided to try

After the 45 min but before adding the media, could clearly see cells as
a pellet in all the centrifuged wells. In addition, the cells in media
that was not centifuged were at the bottom of the u-bottom. However,
after I added the media, the cells "disappeared" meaning that they were
not clearly in the bottom of the well and hard to focus on. The media
was added really carefully along the well wall, so the cells and the
matrix (Cultrex) are definitely in the well.

Suggestion for next time: Cultrex could be diluted with media so there
is no need to add media on top of the droplets.

be in the top parts of the well as the microscopy could not focus high

well was treated with 1 µM Cisplatin within the media and third with 5
µM Cisplatin within the media. Have been taking pictures of the wells
(d0, d5 and d7) but due to issues in cell culture room, I was able to
again take pictures today on 29.10. d13. However, since the organoids
had changed so much between the d7 and d13, I managed to find very few
of the specific spots of spheroids, which I had been following. Took
some pictures before splitting.

1:3 and seeded in 3 wells each with 10 droplets. Well that received ones
Cisplatin 1µM was splitted 1:3 and seeded in 1 well with 10 droplets.

1 well with 10 droplets. So in total 5 wells all growing in M1 media.
Splitted in accordance to the Short format organoid passaging protocol.

To prepare a 7.5 mg/ml Cultrex solution, diluted 865 µl of Cultrex stock
(11.7 mg/ml) in 485 µl cold DPBS.

Splitted in accordance to the Short format organoid passaging protocol.

To prepare a 7.5 mg/ml Cultrex solution, diluted 865 µl of Cultrex stock
(11.7 mg/ml) in 485 µl cold DPBS. Added 750 µl of that solution on top
of the pellet from negative control well and seeded in total 30 x 20 µl
droplets. While washing both of the Cisplatin treated spheroids/cells,
diveded the solution into 1:3 or 1:2. After centrifugation added 250 µl
of Cultrex solution on the pellet and seeded 10 x 20 µl droplets from
both conditions. Incubated 45 min, before adding the media which
included 20 ml M1 media + 10 µl ROCKi.

After 45 min incubation, media was added. However not all droplets had

least amount of time to solidify.

# Sample S494_pOme

**30.10.2024 - day 0 and 31.10.2024 - day 1**

**Copied from Dissociation_protocol_optimization**

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S494_pOme**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

S494_pOme sample was processed together with S495_pOme

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\.

*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

- Washed big tissue pieces once with HBSS.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

f DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - Centrifuged the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

 - Then, it was resuspended in 1 ml media to wait for the other
 > conditions to be at the same step. Then, centrifuged again,
 > and continued with RBC lysis.

 - RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10
 > dilution for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the
 > aliquots for flow cytometry, and freezing.

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++

**Copied from Dissociation_protocol_optimization**

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S497_pOme**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

- Washed big tissue pieces once with HBSS.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\.

shed big tissue pieces once with HBSS.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before, in the middle, and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before, in the middle, and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

# 20.11.2024 Fibroblast culture optimization

Repeated what was done on 22.10.2024.

Used 3 different cell densities: 0.5 E6 cells/ml, 1 E6 cells/ml and 2 E6
cells/ml, and the following conditions that were chosen to this
experiment were: cell suspension centrifuged, cell suspension
resuspended, Cultrex 2.5 mg/ml centrifuged, Cultrex 2.5 mg/ml not
centrifuged, Cultrex 7.5 mg/ml centrifuged and Cultrex 7.5 mg/ml not
centrifuged.

Cells centrifuged means that after the cell suspension is plated, the
plate is centrifuged and then pellet not resuspended into media. Cultrex
centrifuged means that the droplets are made as usually but after plated
the plate is centrifuged.

Used 3 plates of S491_pOme_P2(1/3 P1) after dispase+C&H dissociation
8.11.2024 AE

Used Corning® 96-well Clear Round Bottom Ultra-Low Attachment plate,
Product Number7007

# Sample S505_iAdn

Copied for patient sample protocol:

Tissue was minced in small pieces and dissociated with: 9.7 ml
DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1%
pen/strep) + 200 µl Dispase + 100 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C rotating
for 30 min, vortexed before, in the middle, and after. Filtered through
70 µm cell strainer, RBC lysis with 3 ml for 5 min + 15 ml DMEM/F12+++.
After RBC lysis and centrifugation, the pellet had layers; the bottom
looked normal but the top looked yellowish. Did the RBD lysis again and
refiltered the cells. The final pellet was resuspended in 1 ml of media,
diluted 1:10 for counting and counted.

- Centrifuged = The plate was centrifuged after the content was added
 > to the wells, and the cells were not resuspended in the media/gel
 > solution. In addition, (1) the plate was kept cold so that the
 > Cultrex droplets did not start solidifying until after the
 > centrifugation, and (2) fibrinogen was added carefully to the
 > wells after centrifugation so that the ome-gels/fibrin matrices
 > didn't either solidify before centrifugation.

 > fibrinogen, aprotinin, and thrombin was increased by x2.12

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S506_pAdnR**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

Approaches:

1\.

:**

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S506_pAdnR**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor sample. The piece was huge. Used ⅓ of the
 > whole piece, specifically the part closest to the part where
 > Anastasia took tissue pieces.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before, in the middle, and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transferred the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, washed the stainer with 5 ml media, and discarded the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, washed the stainer with 5 ml media, and discarded the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 5 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 20 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 10 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before, in the middle, and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated first using
Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H). Dispase
dissociation media included 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented
with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 200 µl Dispase + 100
µl DNAse, and the sample was incubated at 37C rotating for 30 min,
vortexed before and after. Suspension was filtered through 70 µm cell
strainer.

The leftover pieces were further dissociated with C&H. The C&H
dissociation media included 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 1 ml C&H+ 100 µl DNAse.
The sample was incubated at 37C rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and
after. After incubation with C&H, the suspension was filtered through a
70 µm cell strainer.

For both cell suspensions (dispase dissociated and dispase+C&H
dissociated) RBC lysis with 2 ml for 5 min + 10 ml DMEM/F12+++, and the
final pellet was resuspended in 1 ml each. From both, a 1:10 dilution
was made for counting.

continued to MACS with the rest (12.9 E6 cells).

A) **Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)**

Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads protocol by Miltenyi Biotec was used as
guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA) to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before using
it.

Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C. The pellet was
resuspended in 103.2 µl of cold MACS buffer (commercial protocol
suggests 80 µl per 10 E6 cells). Added 25.8 µl of Anti-Fibroblast
MicroBeads to the cell suspension (protocol: 20 µl per 10 E6 cells) and
incubated 30 min at RT. Washed the cells with 2 ml MACS buffer and
centrifuged 300 g for 10 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended
in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice. The beads were kept
sterile and therefore worked inside a laminar hood until the incubation.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.

cell suspension onto the column, and lastly, washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS
buffer.

Always wait until all liquid has entered through the column.

The column was removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml
falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly
pushing the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were
flushed out.

Both cell suspensions were centrifuged 300 x for 10 min and then
resuspended in 1 ml FACS buffer. Counted the cells, and cell counts:

## Cell harvesting timepoints

## Summarized results

![](media/image103.png){width="6.5in" height="3.0in"}

Cell viability was dramatically decreasing over time and, because of
this, the experiment was terminated at d7 instead of d3. For the next
experiment, the plating density will be adjusted to 1000 **LIVE**
cells/uL.

Also, all endothelial cells were already dead at d3, so for the next
experiment a positive isolation purification (with anti-fibroblast
beads) will be tested to exclude endothelial cells from the cultures and
perhaps enhance the viability. -\> In a meeting with Anniina this was
disregarded. Anniina does not think it's a big problem to have few
endothelial cells dying in the cultures.

On the other hand, the cancer cell population also seemed to be
declining, however it might be because these cells are forming clumps
that are excluded from the flow analysis (through filtering, and doublet
exclusion). For the next experiment, I will try to take pictures using
the Evos microscope in the cell culture room. Also, it would be nice to
try to count the cells and assess the viability with the cell counter
too.

Calculations were done as follow:

1000 live cells/uL \* 40 uL = 40000 cells/droplet \* 8 droplets/well \*
7 wells (1 extra) =

**2.24 E6 cells are needed in total**

8 droplets/ well \* 7 wells \* 40 uL/ droplet = **2 240 uL are needed in
total**

However, since the omentum gel starts polymerizing as soon as the
fibrinogen is added to the mixture, it is better to make four **(4)**
separate tubes each one of **560 uL** (2240 uL/4) and not everything at
once in a single tube.

We want to keep a cell density of 1 000 live cells/uL, so the total
number of cells per tube that will be needed is:

1 000 cells/uL \* 560 uL = **0.56 E6 cells/tube**

0.56 E6 cells / (2.87 E6 cells/mL\*\*\*) = 0.195 mL/tube = 195.12 uL
**(from the combined cell suspension)**

\*\*\* This was the **LIVE** cell density in the combined cell
suspension (taking into account the mean viability: (viability of
dispase fraction + viability of fibroblasts) / 2)

[The amount of the remaining components per tube will be:]{.mark}

[ORG full medium: (560 uL -- **333.5 uL**) = **226.5 uL of supplemented
media (iPDC media + cytokines)**]{.mark}

To produce these plots, flow cytometry data was first analyzed using
FlowJo. After compensation, gates for time, cells, and live cells were
drawn to: 1) exclude bad quality data, 2) exclude debris, and 3) exclude
dead cells. Next, a CD45 vs EpCAM plot was generated, and the three
resulting populations were used for the "general cell types" plots:

- (CD45+EpCAM- (immune cells)

- CD45-EpCAM+ (cancer cells)

- CD45-EpCAM-(non-immune stromal cells).

For the "specific cell types" plots, the CD45+EpCAM- population was
selected, and a CD8 vs CD3 plot was generated. The three resulting
subpopulations were:

- CD3+CD8- (Non-CD8+ T cells, possibly CD4+ T cells)

- CD3+CD8+ (CD8+ T cells)

- CD3-CD8- (And other immune cells, possibly myeloid cells).

Additionally, the CD45-EpCAM- population was selected, and a histogram
of CD31 expression was plotted. The resulting populations were:

- CD31- (Possibly fibroblasts)

- CD31+ (endothelial cells).

Finally, the percentage of each population (out of all cells in the live
gate) was calculated and saved in an Excel file named
**"S530_pOme_cell_percentages_16042025_MH"**, located in the P Drive:

ntage of each population (out of all cells in the live
gate) was calculated and saved in an Excel file named

For the "specific cell types" plots, the CD45+EpCAM- population was
selected, and a CD8 vs CD3 plot was generated. The three resulting
subpopulations were:

- CD3+CD8- (Non-CD8+ T cells, possibly CD4+ T cells)

- CD3+CD8+ (CD8+ T cells)

- CD3-CD8- (And other immune cells, possibly myeloid cells).

Additionally, the CD45-EpCAM- population was selected, and a histogram
of CD31 expression was plotted. The resulting populations were:

- CD31- (Possibly fibroblasts)

- CD31+ (endothelial cells).

Finally, the percentage of each population (out of all cells in the live

as plotted. The resulting populations were:

gate) was calculated and saved in an Excel file named

Calculations:

10 wells, with each 8 droplets

1 000 **live** cells/µL \* 40 µL = 40 000 cells/droplet

40 000 cells/droplet \* 8 droplets/well \* 11 wells (one extra) = **3
520 000 live cells are needed**

8 droplets/ well \* 11 wells \* 40 µL/ droplet = **3 520 µL are needed
in total**

However, since the omentum gel start polymerizing as soon as the
fibrinogen is added to the mixture, it is better to make **five (5)**
separate tubes each one of 704 µL (3520 µL/5) and not everything at once
in a single tube.

ng as soon as the
fibrinogen is added to the mixture, it is better to make **five (5)**
separate tubes each one of 704 µL (3520 µL/5) and not everything at once
in a single tube.

We want to keep a cell density of 1 000 live cells/µL, so the total
number of cells per tube that will be needed is:

1 000 live cells/µL \* 704 µL = 0.704 E6 live cells/tube

0.704 E6 cells / (3.75 E6 cells/mL) = 188 µL from the original cell

**Aims of the experiment:**

The droplets were allowed to solidify for 1 hour in the incubator and,
after that time, 3.5 mL of iPDC media + cytokines were added into each
well.

**Aims of the experiment:**

- To evaluate the effect of GolgiPlug (Brefeldin A) and GolgiStop
 > (Monensin) on intracellular cytokine retention following T cell
 > activation.

- To assess baseline (unstimulated) cytokine levels and distinguish
 > them from induced levels under different conditions.

**Experimental layout:**

![](media/image21.png){width="6.5in" height="4.416666666666667in"}

**Experimental description:**

- Primary cells were seeded into 6-well plates (Plate 1 and Plate 2)
 > as described above.

- After a few hours of incubation to allow settling, **ImmunoCult™
 > Human T Cell Activator** will be added to selected wells (as
 > indicated in the schematic) and incubated for a total of **24
 > hours**.

- For wells with transport inhibitors, either **GolgiPlug (Brefeldin
 > A)** or **GolgiStop (Monensin)** will be added at the **20-hour
 > mark** (i.e., for the final 4 hours of the activation period), in
 > order to block cytokine secretion and allow intracellular
 > accumulation.

> **ImmunoCult™ Human T Cell Activator:** Suggested amount is 25 uL/mL
> of cell suspension (1E6 cells). In this experiment, the amount will be
> adjusted based on the volume of media (3.5 mL/well), and not the

tivator:** Suggested amount is 25 uL/mL
> of cell suspension (1E6 cells). In this experiment, the amount will be
> adjusted based on the volume of media (3.5 mL/well), and not the
> amount of cells, so **the amount to be added will be 87.5 uL/well**
>
> **GolgiPlug:** Suggested amount is 1 uL/mL of cell suspension (1E6
> cells). In this experiment, the amount will be adjusted based on the
> volume of media, and not the amount of cells, so **the amount to be
> added will be 3.5 uL/well**
>
> **GolgiStop:** Suggested amount is 4 uL/6mL of cell suspension (1E6
> cells). In this experiment, the amount will be adjusted based on the
> volume of media, and not the amount of cells, so **the amount to be
> added will be 2.3 uL/well**

**Experimental notes:**

 > is observed, then this might be a good alternative to consider for
 > a next try.**

**3.11.2025 Dissociation:**

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase,
followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by María and Sarah.

Dispase dissociation media included 67.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12
supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 1.4 ml
Dispase + 700 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed
before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 53.4 ml DMEM/F12+++,
6 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 600 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C,
rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm
cell strainer.

Dispase dissociated cells:

RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. RBC lysis was
repeated with 7 ml for 5 min + 30 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was
resuspended in 20 mL of icPDC media (150 mL of icPDC media was
prepared). For cell counting a 1:10 dilution was used.

Sequentially dissociated cells:

RBC lysis with 7 ml for 5 min + 25 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was
resuspended in 20 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media. For cell counting a 1:10
dilution was used.

break up by scrapping with a cell scrapper and pipetting up and down

aspired using a suction pump and 3.5mL of

break up by scrapping with a cell scrapper and pipetting up and down
several times using a 1 mL pipet before the plate were incubated in cell
culture incubator for 30 min.

Each well was Transfer into 15 mL falcons and washed with 3.5 mL DMEM
F12 +++ media using the 1 mL pipet and additional 3.5 mL DMEM F12 +++
media were added to each falcon. Plates were checked in the microscope,
if still a considerable amount of cells are in the wells, but all were
clean.

All Falcons were centrifugated at 400g for 6 min at +4, supernatant was
removed pellets were resuspended in 800 µl FACS buffer and Falcons of
the same conditions were pooled.

# Sample S102, S134 and S190

#### Objective: LEGENDplex experiment is a bead-based immunoassay to quantify 13 human proteins out of the supernatant from an experiment done by Kursat for the paper Perez-Villatoro et al.

#### 20.11.2025

Conditioned media from the HLA-Dr blocking experiment was collected by
Kursat and frozen at -80 deg until the time of the assay.

On the assay day (20.11.2025), María thawed the samples on ice and

from the HLA-Dr blocking experiment was collected by
Kursat and frozen at -80 deg until the time of the assay.

On the assay day (20.11.2025), María thawed the samples on ice and
performed the assay following the manufacturer's recommended protocol,
assisted by Sarah.

Protocol can be found here:

[[https://www.biolegend.com/Files/Images/media_assets/pro_detail/datasheets/Hu-CD8-Panel-V02_FK.pdf]{.underline}](https://www.biolegend.com/Files/Images/media_assets/pro_detail/datasheets/Hu-CD8-Panel-V02_FK.pdf)

Samples were run in duplicates.

Data was generated using the Fortessa cytometer in the Flow core, on the
same day that the assay was performed.

Data was analyzed using the LegendPlex proprietary software and final
plots were generated using R scripts and averaged data among technical
replicates, and then among patients. During the data analysis, Anniina
informed María via Slack message that the pembro and untreated
conditions were mistakenly switched during the blocking experiment, so
**the labels for these two conditions were switched in the final plots
as per her instructions.**

Raw data, generated plots, and scripts to generate the plots can be
found in P Drive:
P:\\h345\\afarkkilab\\Projects\\NKI\\iPDCs\\MHCII_blockade_LEGENDplex_assay

[06.12.2025 Harvesting:]{.underline}

60 ml of DispaseII solution (1.2 mL DispaseII (stock 50 U/mL) + 600 µl
DNase in 58.2 mL DMEM F12 +++ media ) was prepared.

Medium from wells were aspired using a suction pump and 3.5mL of
DispaseII solution were added into each well. Ome-gel droplets were
break up by scrapping with a cell scrapper and pipetting up and down
several times using a 1 mL pipet before the plate were incubated in cell
culture incubator for 30 min.

Each well was Transfer into 15 mL falcons and washed with 3.5 mL DMEM
F12 +++ media using the 1 mL pipet and additional 3.5 mL DMEM F12 +++
media were added to each falcon. Plates were checked in the microscope,
if still a considerable amount of cells are in the wells, but all were
clean.

All Falcons were centrifugated at 400g for 6 min at +4, supernatant was
removed pellets were resuspended in 800 µl FACS buffer and Falcons of
the same conditions were pooled.

140 ml of DispaseII solution (2.8 mL DispaseII (stock 50 U/mL) + 1.4 mL
DNase in 135.8 mL DMEM F12 +++ media ) was prepared.

Medium from wells were aspired using a suction pump and 3.5mL of
DispaseII solution were added into each well. Ome-gel droplets were
break up by scrapping with a cell scrapper and pipetting up and down
several times using a 1 mL pipet before the plate were incubated in cell
culture incubator for 30 min.

s were
break up by scrapping with a cell scrapper and pipetting up and down

culture incubator for 30 min.

Each well was Transfer into 15 mL falcons containing 3.5 mL DMEM F12 +++
media and washed with 2.5 mL DMEM F12 +++ media using the 1 mL pipet.
All Falcons were centrifugated at 400g for 6 min at +4. 1 mL of TrypleE
was added to each well and incubated for 5 min in the incubator. The
supernatant was removed and pellets were resuspended in the 1 mL of the
corresponding wells of each condition and incubated for 5 min in the
waterbath. 6 mL of DMEM F12 +++ media were added to each Falcon before
centrifugated at 400g for 6 min at +4. Cells were resuspended in FACS
buffer and transferred into eppis for cell counting and Flow cytometry.

**The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase,
followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by Maria and Foteini.

Dispase dissociation media included 18.4 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12

rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm
cell strainer.

2.2026 Day 0**

**Dissociation**

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase,
followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by Maria, Aino and
Foteini.

The sample was divided in two parts and the dispase dissociation
procedure was applied to both parts as follows:

Dispase dissociation media included 29,1 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12
supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 600 µl
Dispase + 300 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed
before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

The Collagenase & Hyaluronidase dissociation procedure was applied to
both parts as follows:

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 26,7 ml DMEM/F12+++,
3 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 300 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C,
rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm
cell strainer.

Dispase dissociated cells:

\(1\) First RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. Second
RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet
(part) was resuspended in 20 mL of icPDC media (1:5 dilution) for
experiment. The final pellet (part) was resuspended in 20 mL of
DMEM/F12+++ media (1:10 dilution) for Biobanking.

Sequentially dissociated cells: (1) First RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5
min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. Second RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml

++ media (1:10 dilution) for Biobanking.

Sequentially dissociated cells: (1) First RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5
min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. Second RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml
DMEM/F12+++ (1:5 dilution). The final pellet was resuspended in 20 mL of
DMEM/F12+++ media.

> **Note:** Penicillin-Streptomycin, 10000 U/mL (#15140122, GibcoLife
> Technologies, was not included in the DMEM/F12+++ media.

**Magnetic Activated Cell Sorting MACs**

**Negative Isolation**

Performed negative isolation for the obtained sequentially dissociated
cells.

**Negative isolation:** Depleting the sample from cancer and immune
cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and
anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in
a QuadroMACS separator.

CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45 MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi
Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: (29,73 x 5 = 148,65 )ml DPBS (w/o
MgCl2 and CaCl2) + 750 µl FBS + 600 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM
EDTA in DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered
before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

FBS + 600 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM
EDTA in DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered
before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Based on the cell count, divide the cell suspension into equal fractions
so that the maximum number of cells does not exceed the recommended
sample size. Recommended sample size for leukocytes: 0.1 E6 - 100 E6
labeled cells in 10 E6 - 2000 E6 total cells per column. Four (4) equal
fractions were calculated, requiring 2.208 µl of cold MACS buffer for a
total of 240E6 cells, specifically 60E6 cells per column.

Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 2.208 µl of cold MACS buffer
(calculation: the desired volume \[500 µl\] - the volume of both beads
\[2x 20 µl each type\]). Added 480 µl of both anti-EpCAM and 480 µl
anti-CD45 beads, respectively, to the cell suspension and incubated 30
min at +4C shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 24 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm (408 g)
for 5 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500µl x 4
(fractions) of MACS buffer.

Placed three LS columns and 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS
Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then
applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml

nd 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS
Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then
applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml
MACS buffer.

Columns were removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon
tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly pushing
the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed
out.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged 1500 rpm g for 5 min. Resuspended
the flow through cells in 5mL icPDC media. Resuspended the column bound
cells in 10 mL DMEM/F12+++ media. Cells in icPDC media
(fibroblast-enriched fraction) were then subjected to dead cell removal
(see next section), while the cells resuspended in DMEM/F12+++ (cancer
and immune cell-enriched fraction) were used for biobanking (see
biobanking section).

**[Dead Cell Removal]{.underline}**

Dead cell depletion: cells are magnetically labeled with Dead Cell
Removal MicroBeads and passed through a separation column. The unlabeled
living cells run through.

Overview:

1. Prepare 1x Binding Buffer by dilution of 20x Binding Buffer Stock
 > Solution with sterile, double-distiled water, 25 mL final volume
 > of 1x Binding Buffer was prepared, 1.25 mL of Binding Buffer +
 > 23.75mL of ddH2O.

2. Collect cells, centrifuge at 300 g and remove supernatant
 > completely.

3. Resuspend cells with Dead cell Removal Microbeads, 541.4 μl beads
 > accounting for 54.14E6 cells. Mix well and incubate for 15 min at
 > room temperature.

4. Place column in a MACs Separator. Prepare by rinsing with 1x Binding
 > Buffer.

5. Apply cell suspensions onto the column. Rinse with 1x Binding
 > Buffer.

6. Collect effluent as live cell fraction.

Followed the protocol according to the ventor:
[[https://static.miltenyibiotec.com/asset/150655405641/document_9ndq9pouph0pvf6lmr5osgv41s?content-disposition=inline]{.underline}](https://static.miltenyibiotec.com/asset/150655405641/document_9ndq9pouph0pvf6lmr5osgv41s?content-disposition=inline)

to non-immune stroma-enriched fraction was 1:1. -- 9.6E6 cells x

 > centrifuged at 450 rcf for 6 min at 4 °C.

- Finally, cells were resuspended in FACS buffer and stored at 4 °C,
 > protected from light.

> there was no spectral overlap.
>
> 3\. For running samples, the FSC and SSC voltages for beads were
> adjusted to 140 and 230, respectively.

washed twice with 1 mL of DMEM/F12+++ medium, and the washes were

quently transferred into 15 mL Falcon tubes

Insights into tumor-host interactions in high-grade serous ovarian cancer using a 3D model of the tumor ecosystem

INTRODUCTION

High-grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC) is the most lethal form of ovarian cancer, with treatment resistance often driven by complex interactions within the tumor microenvironment (TME). Conventional 2D models lack the cellular diversity and structural context needed to study these interactions, limiting their ability to predict patient responses.

nvironment (TME). Conventional 2D models lack the cellular diversity and structural context needed to study these interactions, limiting their ability to predict patient responses. To address this, we developed a patient-derived 3D model of HGSC that includes cancer cells, immune cells, and cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) embedded in a human omentum-derived extracellular matrix (ECM). This model better recapitulates the TME and provides a platform to explore drug responses and identify predictive biomarkers.

1

Rationale behind our ADC selection

List of ADCs from Selleckchem (and also MedChemExpress)

First filtered the list for relevance to HGSC

In case someone is interested

Possible experimental controls

5

Aino’s (Thesis) gating strategy:

Exclude debris: Gate out small particles or cell fragments that are not relevant to the analysis.

Select single cells: Ensure only individual cells are analyzed, excluding doublets or aggregates.

Gate live cells: Exclude dead cells using the live/dead marker.

Identify major populations using:

EpCAM⁺ → Cancer cells
CD45⁺ → Leukocytes
EpCAM⁻ CD45⁻ → Non-immune stroma
EpCAM- CD45- CD31+ → Endothelial cells
EpCAM- CD45- CD31- FAP+/a-SMA+/PDRGF-b+/CD29+ → Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs)

6

Possible ADC readout: Binding assay

With therapeutic antibodies gating is straightforward, but not with ADCs … so, we could use:

7

e.g., Alexa Fluor 647 or PerCP-Cy5.5

Note: Secondary antibody should not bind anything else, especially Fc receptors on immune cells. So, we may need an Fc block to prevent nonspecific binding (which we already have, I think).

Possible ADC readout: Toxicity assay

Tumor cells should be the primary ADC target

But ADCs may cause a bystander effect, where the payload diffuses into nearby non-target cells (CAF, immune cells), so we want to detect also cell death/apoptosis 

Inside each population, we could gate for:

X axis: Annexin V (e.g., AF700) (Note: Annexin V Binding Buffer (contains Ca²⁺))
Y axis: DCM (viability dye)

And then interpret Quadrants as:

Annexin− / DCM− → Live (healthy)
Annexin+ / DCM− → Early apoptotic
Annexin+ / DCM+ → Late apoptotic / necrotic
Annexin− / DCM+ → Necrotic / membrane-compromised

8

Issue: additional titration needed for

9

Unconjugated Antibody

Median Fluorescence Intensity (MFI) increase in EpCAM+ cells (tumor cells) vs EpCAM− (CAF, immune) tells you your unconjugated antibody is binding specifically.

Fluorescent Anti-Human IgG

MFI on EpCAM+ (tumor cells) vs secondary-only (background control) vs EpCAM−/CD45+ (immune cells)

Fc-block

MFI of CD45+ should drop after blocking, as Fc receptors are only on immune cells

Thank you. Open for input! ☺️
